# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an S3 bucket that stores user-uploaded files and application backups. Allow full read/write access for our application IAM role. Keep the configuration simple - we'll add versioning and lifecycle rules in the next iteration.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: S3 bucket for user-uploaded files and application backups.

Resources:
  UserFilesAndBackupsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: your-company-app-data-bucket-unique-name # Replace with a globally unique name
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: Development
        - Key: Project
          Value: YourApplication
        - Key: DataClassification
          Value: ApplicationData
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true

  # IAM Policy to allow access for the application IAM role
  # Attach this policy to your application's IAM role (e.g., EC2 instance profile, ECS task role, Lambda execution role)
  ApplicationBucketAccessPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: YourApplicationS3BucketAccessPolicy
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Action:
              - s3:GetObject
              - s3:PutObject
              - s3:DeleteObject
              - s3:ListBucket
            Resource:
              - !GetAtt UserFilesAndBackupsBucket.Arn
              - !Sub "${UserFilesAndBackupsBucket.Arn}/*"
      # Users:
      #   - !Ref ApplicationUser
      # Roles:
      #   - !Ref ApplicationRole
      # Groups:
      #   - !Ref ApplicationGroup
      # Uncomment and replace with the ARN of your application's IAM role or user
      # For demonstration, it's left without a direct attachment.
      # In a real scenario, you'd attach this to an existing Role via !Ref or !GetAtt.
      # Example for an existing role:
      Roles:
        - !Ref YourApplicationIAMRoleLogicalId # Replace with the logical ID of your application's IAM role

Outputs:
  BucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 bucket
    Value: !Ref UserFilesAndBackupsBucket
  BucketArn:
    Description: ARN of the S3 bucket
    Value: !GetAtt UserFilesAndBackupsBucket.Arn
